title: EEG-based Voice Conversion : Hearing the Voice of Your Brain
abstract: The connection between Electroencephalography (EEG) signals and human voice has gained significant attention, with studies demonstrating the feasibility of speech synthesis from EEG data. However, EEG-based voice conversion (VC) remains largely unexplored. To address this, we present the first EEG-based zero-shot VC system that converts speech into a target speakerâs voice without prior target data. Our method integrates an EEG feature extraction module with an alignment module to map EEG features to speaker-specific voice features. By leveraging an innovative three-stage training strategy and a pre-trained VC modelâtrained solely on speech dataâwe achieve zero-shot conversion. Experiments on the Single-Word-Production Dutch-iBIDS dataset confirm the systemâs ability to reliably convert speech to a target speakerâs voice. This work highlights the potential of EEG-based VC for advancing assistive communication and brain-computer interfaces. All demos are available in https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15510829.
relevance_note: First EEG-based zero-shot voice conversion system; advances novel BCI-based VC.
